sources.notes: Randomly permute arithmetic operations and compute expected final value modulo 1e9+7.
Official solution normalizes subtraction/division and computes expected multiplier suffix products with DP.
Cross-check for merging additive operations and elementary symmetric DP.

annotation.summary: Given an initial value and operations +, -, *, /, compute the expected result after applying a uniformly random permutation of the operations.
annotation.constraints: n <= 3000 and sum n^2 <= 3000^2, so O(m^2) DP over multiplicative operations fits.
annotation.core_idea: Normalize '-' into adding a negative value and '/' into multiplying by modular inverse. Multiplicative operations commute, and additive operations can be combined by linearity of expectation. If exactly k multipliers appear after an additive term, their average product is the kth elementary symmetric sum divided by C(m,k), and the insertion position contributes probability 1/(m+1). Compute all elementary symmetric sums with one-dimensional DP.
annotation.complexity: O(m^2 + n log MOD), where m is the number of multiplication/division operations.

solution_variants.name: elementary-symmetric-expectation-dp
solution_variants.summary: Normalize operations, compute elementary symmetric sums of multipliers, and average suffix products by subset size.

solution_variants.name: divide-and-conquer-polynomial-product
solution_variants.summary: For larger constraints, multiply polynomials (1 + b_i z) by divide-and-conquer or NTT-style batching to get the same coefficients faster.
solution_variants.complexity: O(m log^2 m) with suitable polynomial multiplication

tags.evidence: The solution computes expected contributions over all random operation permutations.
A one-dimensional DP computes elementary symmetric sums of the multiplicative factors.
The expected multiplier uses elementary symmetric sums, binomial denominators, and averaging over positions.
Division and expected fractions are evaluated modulo 1e9+7 using modular inverses.
